entry.title: Unable to Read Repository at / Error Connecting to URL Error In Eclipse IDE-SAP BTP / S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Everyone , 

For the past few months / weeks I have been getting either of these two errors  

1. Error connecting to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ . 

2. unable to read repository  https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest or URL https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/<eclipse-version> . 

while installing the ADT Tools in the eclipse . 

I have extensively seen this error in the 2024-12 version of the IDE. 

there are many solutions being discussed around but I have found a rather simple solution , which I would like to share .

Steps : 

1. Go to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ for getting the ADT tools repository for your eclipse version .

2. select the repository  Link which you will be using to install the eclipse , in my case it will be https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2024-12 .

3. Open the link in a new tab.

4. Save the Certificate for this site ; I'm using the Google Chrome browser , I will mention the steps for Microsoft Edge Below in this Blog .

  1. Click on the view site information icon (Left to the YRL) 

  2. Click on the Connection is Secure menu   

  3. Click on Certificate is Valid option 

  4. Go to the Details tab and click on Export button 

  5. Save the certificate with a name 

5. Apply the saved Certificate .

  1. Go to the saved Location and double click the certificate . 

  2. Click on Install Certificate  and choose Current User Click Next 

  3. Choose Automatically select the certificate store based on type of certificate option and choose next 

  4. Click Finish 

  5. You will get a popup saying import is done successfully  , Click Ok 

6. Now restart the eclipse and follow the steps to install ADT tools .

 

Steps to Save Certificate in Microsoft Edge Browser:

Once you are in the ADT tools link Page (Now you are in step 4)

  1. Click on the lock icon left to the URL 

  2. Click on the connection is secure menu 

  3. Click on the certificate button next to close button 

  4. Go to the Details tab and Click on Export button 

  5. Save your certificate 

  6. Now resume from step 5 

NB : This Blog is written solely from my experience , knowledge and my vision . If any misleading and wrong information is conveyed please correct me in the comments.

#Knowledge_sharing_is_caring

entry.title: Free SAP BTP - Create new Subaccount
entry.content:
This article will show the creation of a new Subaccount in your recent SAP BTP global.

In order to create a new Subaccount, you need to be at the global account level (green rectangle) and then open the Create menu, selecting "Subaccount" (yellow ellipse).

A new screen will open, asking for important information about the new Subaccount, pay attention to the Region.

The region will have a BIG influence on what you can do (for free) in your SAP BTP account, I recommend taking a look at this link below to find out what are free services available in the region.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/viewServices

In the spoiler there is a small branch in the subject, but necessary for future peace of mind.... feel free to sneak peek 

Spoiler

There is a service called "Landscape Portal" responsible for admin tasks at SAP BTP, like Hana hibernation, support users, etc... I found out the hard way that this service is only available in EUR (cf-eu10 or cf-eu11), after getting crazy looking the region I choose ( Azure US East).

My advice is this service will be used every day to start the Hana system (I will explain why in the next article), this switch takes only 3 clicks. For us developers who are concerned about the new development performance, LAG, and possible expansion, look for the region closest to you. I just created another Subaccount in eu10 and everything else close to me in USA/Texas.

After the SAP BTP process the creation of your new Subaccount, you can go into it and create the new services (remember to choose free ones).

Also, now is a good time to back to the main global account and take a look into the finance part (Cost and Usage).

Here you can check and fix potential cost associate to you account. We are trying to stay at free tier / no-cost.

If you missed the 1st article how to create your global account, you can navigate to it below or go to next article showing how to create / deploy your first service.

 

< Previous  Next >

entry.title: End-to-End Guide: Deploying a Fiori App – From BTP Destination Creation to Public Cloud Deployment
entry.content:
Overview

Deploying an application in the public cloud requires a structured approach involving multiple configurations and integrations. This guide provides a detailed, step-by-step process to ensure a smooth deployment. The key steps include setting up destination connections, creating communication systems, building the app in Business Application Studio (BAS), deploying it, and configuring it in ABAP. This document also highlights important screenshot points to help visualize each step.

Step 1: Set Up the Destination Connection

  1. Open BTP Cockpit and Login: Access the BTP cockpit and log in to your account.
  2. Navigate to Sub-Account: Select and go to the relevant sub-account.
  3. Go to Destinations: Navigate to the "Destinations" section and create a new destination.
  4. Configure Destination Settings:
    • URL: Copy the hostname URL from the Communication Systems app and paste it into the destination URL field. Make sure that to use the URL of the development client to build and deploy an application. If the URL of customizing client is used, then it will lead to issues while deploying application.
    • Authentication Type: Use SAMLAssertion.
    • Audience: Retrieve the OAuth 2.0 SAML2 Audience from the technical data section in the Communication Systems app.

       

    • AuthnContextClassRef: Set it to urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PreviousSession.
    • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
    • HTML5.Timeout: 60000
    • nameIdFormat: urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
    • sap-client: Specify your client (e.g., 100).
    • WebIDEEnabled: true
    • WebIDESystem: Specify your system (e.g., KOE).
    • WebIDEUsage: dev_abap, odata_abap, bsp_execute_abap, ui5_execute_abap.

       

  5. Download Trust File: Click on "Download Trust" to save the trust configuration locally.

Step 2: Create a Communication System in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access the Communication Systems App: Open the app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

     

  2. Create a New Communication System:
    • Specify the system ID as BAS_<Subdomain Name> and provide a meaningful system name.

       

  3. Set Technical Data:
    • Go to the "Technical Data" section and select the checkbox for "Inbound Only".

       

    • Go to the identity provider and turn the SAML Bearer Assertion provider.

       

  4. Configure Identity Provider:
    • Enable the "SAML Bearer Assertion" provider.
    • Upload the trust file downloaded in the previous step.
    • After uploading the document fill the above SAML Bearer Issuer field by coping the address from the Signing certificate Subject from the “cfapps….” to till the last.

Step 3: Create the Application in BAS

  1. Open BAS: Access the SAP Business Application Studio.
  2. Create Application Using Template:
    • Select the "SAP Fiori Generator" template.
  3. Configure Application:
    • Select the template as "List Report Page" or required template.

       

    • Choose the data source, system, and OData service.

       

    • Select the main entity of the service.

       

  4. Add Project Attributes:
    • Provide details like project name, namespace, and deployment configurations.

       

       
  5. Add Deployment Configuration:
    • Add the required details for deployment.

       

  6. Configure Fiori Launchpad:
    • Add the required details for the launchpad configuration.

       

  7. Finalize and Create Project: Click "Next" and create the project.

Step 4: Deploy the App from BAS

  1. Open Integrated Terminal:
    • Right-click on the project folder and open the terminal.
  2. Run Deployment Command:
    • Execute "npm run deploy" in the terminal.

       

    • Verify details and confirm deployment by typing y. 
  3. Verify Deployment: Ensure the deployment completes successfully.

     

  4. After deploying the application, if you see the preview of the application, you will get a 404 error. That is because the deployed app should be configured and published in IAM apps in Eclipse.

Step 5: Configure the App in ABAP (Eclipse)

  1. Open Eclipse: Launch Eclipse and open the package used for deployment.
  2. Create IAM App:
    • Right-click on the package and create an IAM app.

       

    • Provide name, description, and set Application Type to "External App".
    • Assign a transport request (TR) and finish the setup.

       

  3. Add Fiori Launchpad App ID:
    • Locate the app ID under the package: Fiori User Interface > Launchpad App Descriptor Items.
    • Add this ID to the IAM app configuration.

       

  4. Create Business Catalog:
    • Create and assign the app to a new business catalog.

       

    • Click on next and select the TR and click on next.
    • Then the dialog opens for creating the Business catalog app assignment, set the name and description and select next and choose the TR.

       

  5. Publish the Catalog and App:
    • Publish the business catalog locally.
    • Publish the IAM app. 
  6. Check the preview of the application form the deployed link.

Step 6: Assign the App to Users

  1. Verify Published IAM App and Catalog:
    • Open the "Display IAM App" to check the published app.

       

    • Verify the catalog in the "Business Catalogs" app.

       

  2. Create Spaces and Pages:
    • Define spaces and pages in the Fiori launchpad.
    • Assign the app to a role and add users to the role.
  3. Verify User Access: Ensure the app is available on the user’s home screen.

Summary

This guide walks you through deploying an SAP Fiori app in the public cloud, covering destination setup, BAS development, deployment, and ABAP configuration. Step-by-step instructions with screenshot placeholders ensure clarity. Following this process minimizes errors for a smooth deployment.

entry.title: View Business Event Logs
entry.content:
This analytical application lets you review business events logged in Business Event Logging.

Screen Areas

Information filter

You can use the information filter at the top of the screen to select which data is displayed in the chart and table. This allows you to view your business event log data in various ways. You must enter the Object and Time Interval, as they are mandatory filters. Additionally, you can use other filters such as “Object Component,” “User,” and so on. The Producer Namespace helps you differentiate between SAP-provided and customer events. SAP-provided business events always carry the producer namespace “sap.s4.beh.”

Fig. 2: Filter bar

Visual filter

Visual filter provides a graphical interface for interacting with and filtering your data. It allows you to view and filter the frequency of events based on "Year and Calendar Week," as well as by "Object Component," such as Item or Schedule Line.

Fig. 3: Visual filter bar

Bar chart

The bar chart offers a concise overview of the frequency of specific events. By selecting a bar, you can filter the entries in the table. Additionally, utilize the View By filter to explore different dimensional data. You also have the option to change the chart type from the default bar chart to other types of charts.

Fig. 4: Bar chart in Hybrid view

Fig. 5: Bar chart in Chart-only view

The "Hybrid view," also referred to as "Chart and Table View," is the default setting that displays both the chart and the table simultaneously. Additionally, you have the option to toggle between "Chart-only view" and "Table-only view" within the chart area.

Fig. 6: Toolbar in chart area

Table

The table located at the bottom of the screen provides additional details regarding the logged business events, including Business Event Type, Object IDs, Performed By, and Producer Namespace. In the "Settings" button, you have the option to customize the displayed columns to include information such as Object Keys, Node ID, Source of Data, and more. Additionally, you can utilize the "Group By" and "Sort by" features for the columns.

Upon selecting a table entry, you will be directed to the details page of the business event. Selecting the Object IDs gives you an overview of all the events for an object (components), and the Performed By column provides access to the user contact card.

Fig. 7: Table in Table-only view

Details page

The specifics of the event are presented on the object page. This page provides a summary of the key properties associated with the event.

  • Event type and version
  • Object component and IDs
  • Date of execution
  • User
  • Version and release status of the event (mostly “released” and “deprecated”
  • Source system
  • Transaction codes, if available
  • Source of Data: currently only “Business Event” = externally released business events by SAP, or customer events with event binding

A raised event may include Event Qualifiers and Field Changes, which are presented in separate tables.

Event qualifiers are occasionally used in generic business events. Their values can offer more precise semantics to the business event.

Field Changes provides a comprehensive list of all value modifications recorded in the logged business events. For events with a deep structure, this table additionally displays the path to the changed field.

EventUUID serves as a distinct identifier for the event, while NodeID identifies a specific instance of an object component, such as a Sales Order Item. Additionally, the Transaction ID denotes the technical session(SAP LUW) during which the event was executed within the system.

Fig. 8: Details page

To enable logging of all business event data in the BEL logging configuration, check the All Data option for the SAP Object Type. You can access the logged business event data by clicking the "View Business Event Data" button on the details page.

Fig. 9: Business event data in a popup

Release Information

 

ProductAvailability
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition2411 and later
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition2021 FPS02* and later
SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise)2021 FPS02* and later
SAP BTP ABAP Environment2411

* In SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, the functionality of logging and viewing all business data is only available in 2023.

Fiori Apps Library Reference

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S34

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29PCE

SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise): https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29OP

If you are involved in an incentive management implementation of any size, you don’t want to miss this session. Read more

entry.title: ABAP new syntax 7.5+ - REDUCE with COND operator
entry.content:
REDUCE is a powerful operator for reduction to a specific result by iterating a set of values. When it is used with COND, it can open a new door for coding.

In older syntax, if we have to calculate/deduce something from any internal table with some data, LOOP-ENDLOOP is the first go to option. But now let's see the power of REDUCE with COND operator.

Let's take an example, we have a below table where we have CREDIT/DEBIT entries for documents as shown below. Now, we have to calculate short-falling balance to clear this document, debit will be subtracted and credit will be added. 

Document NumberCredit(H)/Debit(S)Amount
1000000112S101,111.99
1000000112H202,222.98
1000000112S303,333.97
1000000112H404,444.96
1000000112S202222.20

 

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_tab,
          belnr TYPE belnr_d,
          shkzg TYPE shkzg,
          dmbtr TYPE dmbtr,
        END OF ty_tab,
        tt_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_tab.

DATA : lt_tab    TYPE tt_tab,
       lv_amount TYPE dmbtr.

lt_tab =  VALUE #( ( belnr = '1000000112'  shkzg = 'S' dmbtr = '101111.99' )
                   ( belnr = '1000000112'  shkzg = 'H' dmbtr = '202222.98' )
                   ( belnr = '1000000112'  shkzg = 'S' dmbtr = '303333.97' )
                   ( belnr = '1000000112'  shkzg = 'H' dmbtr = '404444.96' )
                   ( belnr = '1000000112'  shkzg = 'S' dmbtr = '202222.20' ) ).

 

Old way of calculating short-fall amount:

 

LOOP AT lt_tab INTO DATA(ls_tab) WHERE belnr = '1000000112'.
  IF ls_tab-shkzg = 'S'. "debit
    lv_amount = lv_amount - ls_tab-dmbtr.
  ELSEIF ls_tab-shkzg = 'H'. "credit
    lv_amount = lv_amount + ls_tab-dmbtr.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

 

New way of calculating short-fall amount:

 

lv_amount = REDUCE #( INIT a TYPE dmbtr
                      FOR wa IN lt_tab
                      WHERE ( belnr = '1000000112' )
                      NEXT a = COND #( WHEN wa-shkzg = 'S' THEN a - wa-dmbtr
                                       WHEN wa-shkzg = 'H' THEN a + wa-dmbtr ) ).

 

 

Let me take another example, we want to know whether all customer names with search string contains same customer number or not. Try to comment/uncomment codeline 11 and see the results. 

Customer NameCustomer Number
NESTLE BR1001143
NESTLE ARG1001143
NESTLE COFFEE1001143
NESTLE BRUSSELS1001145
NESTLE
1001143

 

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_cust,
          name1 TYPE name1,
          kunnr TYPE kunnr,
        END OF ty_cust,
        tt_cust TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_cust.

DATA : lt_cust    TYPE tt_cust.
lt_cust =  VALUE #( ( name1 = 'NESTLE BR'     kunnr = '1001143' )
                    ( name1 = 'NESTLE ARG'    kunnr = '1001143' )
                    ( name1 = 'NESTLE COFFEE' kunnr = '1001143' )
*                    ( name1 = 'NESTLE BRUSSELS' kunnr = '1001145' )
                    ( name1 = 'NESTLE'        kunnr = '1001143' ) ).

DATA(lv_diffcust) = REDUCE i( INIT i = 0
                              FOR wa IN lt_cust
                              WHERE ( name1 CP 'NESTLE*' )
                              NEXT i = COND #( WHEN wa-kunnr = lt_cust[ 1 ]-kunnr THEN i
                                               ELSE i + 1 ) ).
IF lv_diffcust <> 0.
  WRITE : 'Anyone is having different customer number'.
ELSE.
  WRITE : 'All are having same customer number'.
ENDIF.

 

There can be many more variations. Above examples are just to illustrate how easily we can deduce something conditionally by using REDUCE with COND operator.

That's a little stuff which can enhance your deduction technique.
Feedbacks are always welcome.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2505
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 22 days* in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.

* Starting with 2508 upgrade, a pre-upgrade period of 4 weeks is planned.   

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2505 is planned for May 17/18.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on April 25 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on April 18. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

Milestones for release 2505:

  • March 19: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • April 19: Announcement of the planned new features
  • April 22: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • April 25: Pre-upgrade
  • May 14: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • May 17/18: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by April 21 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on April 25 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by May 2 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, May 14 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Decoding SAP Fields: VBAK-BSTNK vs. VBKD-BSTKD 🕵️‍♀️
entry.content:
Think of SAP like a giant digital filing cabinet for a business. It has many folders (we call them "tables") to keep everything organized.

VBAK - The "Header" Folder 📂: Imagine the main folder for each Sales Order. `VBAK` is a table that holds the header data of a sales document. This is like the cover sheet of a file, containing key summary info for the entire order.
VBKD - The "Business Details" Folder 📁: Think of a sub-folder within the Sales Order file for business-related details. `VBKD` is a table storing the business data for a sales document. This holds more specific details about the business side of the order.

Now, let's decode the codes you asked about:

VBAK-BSTNK: This refers to the field called `BSTNK` inside the `VBAK` table. `BSTNK` stands for "Purchase Order Number" (from your Customer!). So, `VBAK-BSTNK` is essentially the Customer's Purchase Order Number recorded at the header level of the sales order.
VBKD-BSTKD: This refers to the field called `BSTKD` inside the `VBKD` table. Guess what? `BSTKD` also stands for "Purchase Order Number"! `VBKD-BSTKD` is also the Customer's Purchase Order Number, but recorded within the business data section of the sales order.

So, What's the Difference? 🤔

Essentially, both `VBAK-BSTNK` and `VBKD-BSTKD` are designed to capture the same thing: the customer's purchase order number.

Why are they in different places (tables)? SAP is structured to organize data efficiently. Having the PO number in both the header (`VBAK`) and business data (`VBKD`) tables can be useful for different purposes within the system, like reporting or specific business process logic. It's a bit like having a label on the main folder and also on a specific document inside - redundancy for easier access depending on what you're doing!

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_decoding-sap-fields-vbak-bstnk-vs-vbkd-bstkd-activity-7305904682978234368-uAph?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAJPDL8BjpJe3thZB6t04k7Z2hRVHhboAec

entry.title: Step by Step Implementation of Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
entry.content:
Creating Customer Events for Customer Business Objects 

Context:  

In the earlier blog we introduced various scenarios in which business events can be extended and logged via Business Event Logging. In this blog we will explain the steps needed to implement a custom scenario with all its variations.  

Environment:  

This feature is available for customers working with the SAP BTP ABAP environment, but it also works for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private, and S/4HANA (On-Premises) editions from release 2022 FPS 2 onwards. 

Pre-requisites: 

To get started, ensure that you have the following roles:  

You must also create 2 custom roles an example is given below:  

  •  ZBEL_CONFIG using the template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT to configure Business Event Logging. 
  •  ZBEL_MONITORING using the template SAP_BR_BUSINESS_PROCESS_SPEC to check the event log. 

A role created using the template SAP_BR_DEVELOPER is also needed to create the required ABAP artifacts for this scenario. 

You'll also need the ADT for Eclipse IDE 

 Explanation: For this example, we use a customer RAP Business object with the following structure:

This is a simple RAP Business Object with a Header Node and Item Node and set of attributes corresponding to each node. 

To cover Business Event Logging related scenarios, we have defined two item-related events ItemStatusChanged and ItemQtyChanged and one header level event StatusChanged. All these events transport field change information. 

The equivalent Behaviour definition (BDEF) looks like below  

Modelling Event Parameter:   

From the BDEF we see that each event has a deep parameter assigned to it. This parameter stands for the event payload format. The deep format is used here, as it is the standard way to transport field changes.  

For the event parameter ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED for event ItemQtyChanged, the event parameter represents the event payload (the business data beyond the key). The payload is modelled using abstract entity. The abstract entity ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED is a nested structure with certain fields and a node named as __before (two underscores) of type ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGEDOLD. This is the default way to define field changes in a business event. 

Both the structures have the same fields. The structure ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED holds the latest values of Quantity and UOM while ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGEDOLD holds the values of Quantity and UOM before the change was executed.  

Please note that you are required to maintain the annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in abstract entities as well as the RAP CDS views. 

 

Below is an illustration of how the event parameters are filled and event raised in the BDEF implementation class. 

 

SAP Object Type and SAP Object Node Type 

 

For each node we need to create the SAP Object Node Type and for header node we create SAP Object Type as well in ADT as shown in below picture. The names should be maintained in CamelCase.  

 

Event Binding: Maintain the event bindings using a customer namespace. 

Business Event Modelling in On-Premise/ Private Cloud Prior 2023 Release: 

In the releases prior to 2023, the event modelling was done in different way as RAP framework didn't allow declaration of a given node event at the respective node. All events had to be declared at the root node level in BDEF. BEL supports logging of the events in this scenario as well, but we need to follow few rules.  

 

 

Corresponding BDEF: 

For this alternate example we have created SAP Object Types and SAP Object Node Type 

SAP Object Type.           =  ZBusinessObjectHeaderAlt 

SAP Object Node Type = ZBusinessObjectItemAlt 

A careful look at the abstract entity definition you will notice three important distinctions. 

 

 

  1. It is mandatory to maintain annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in all the abstract entity with the correct name. 

     2. For the node level events Event.sapObjectNodeTypeKey annotation need to be maintained with the                  correct names of the Key Names for that node. 

      3. To model the old and new values in abstract entity we need to use event.previousValue.element                       annotation. 

  This functionality is available on:   

          ABAP on BTP (Steampunk)  

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2022 FPS2 onwards)     

         SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise 2022 FPS2 onwards  

Note: In On-Premise and Private Cloud Editions for release 2022, the functionality to create SAP Object Type (SOT) and SAP Object Node Type (SONT) is not available for customer. The customer can explicitly define SOT in the event binding and SONT in the annotations. Care should be taken that the names are in camel case. 

Once all the above considerations are met in a RAP BO we can use BEL  by a simple configuration. We need to maintain SAP Object Type via the steps given below and select Activate Logging and Log All Data Options. With this we are good to go with logging our custom business events using BEL. 

 

Execution and Monitoring:  

For the demo purpose I have created an ABAP code to consume the RAP BO using EML thereby triggering these events multiple times. We can visualize these logs using BEL apps. 

 

Display Business Event Log App 

Customer events can always be identified via the entry in the Producer Namespace Column.  

Detail View of Item Quantity Changed 

The source of the demo example is provided at the below given git repo.

https://github.com/aarsaraf/DemoExample.git

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #22
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • April 29th, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Iwona Hahn (BTP ABAP Product Management)
    • Johannes Heinrich (Development Expert, ABAP DDIC/CDS)
    • Matthias Herchenroether (Product Owner ABAP CDS
Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • CDS Table Entities: the new way to model database tables in ABAP incl. demo
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Software 3.0 and us - My vision of GenAI wave for us SAP developers
entry.content:
Having been a technical consultant and Architect myself and also having built a consulting practice around technology, it is very much of my interest to understand how GenAI will affect our jobs, SAP technical consultants.

For this reason, I decided to lay out here my impressions and my understanding of what is happening and (again, my vision) of what may end up happening.

 

From Software 1.0 to software 3.0.

 

Software 1.0 is considered the classical programming we are all familiar with. Add here most programming languages. We code instructions for the machine to perform. Precise, exact, predictable.

I can’t pinpoint who created the term “Software 2.0”but it is widely recognized that Andrej Karpathy made it very popular. Link here https://karpathy.medium.com/software-2-0-a64152b37c35

In short, software 2.0 is the modelization of Neural Networks. We don’t give precise instructions. Machine learns from data. This is a very different story. We build a model to support the identification of cat photos and load so many pictures that at one point the “machine”, the model will predict with enough confidence if the given picture is or not a cat. It is radically different.

Jump forward to Large Language models, these ALSO have been structured on top of neural networks to “predict the next token”(sorry, had to add this as a joke, this expression is used sometimes to undermine the capabilities of LLMs. We know it is more than that, but at the same time, it is not wrong either)

Now, a new term is emerging, software 3.0 and this is where I would like to focus on in this BLOG.

Software 3.0 the composition of new applications/workflows/agents/teams of agents based on LLMs. In short, it may be a python or an ABAP application that is calling LLM’s at one point of another. A lot like the mashups of the early days, this composition programming is what I believe the new upcoming reality of programming, at least for genai based applications.

But this domain has very special and new techniques (RAG, Advanced RAG, Agentic systems, teams of agents, long and short term memory, tools/skills).

 

So, lets get to this: Who is this for and how will it work

 

GenAI, building the tool vs using it

 

An important distintion (and directly related to the difference in what is called Software 2.0 and 3.0) is that, you can work at building the LLMs (very few people work in this space, statistically) or fine-tuning (still, not necessarily a very often used technique). Or you can work a leveraging an existing LLM to build a GenAI powered application (we are back to programming)

 

 

Skills required

 

Software 1.0, as baseline requires development knowledge and skillls. They tend to be language specific (an ABAPer is an ABAPer, he may or maynot be able to code in PYTHON, if he can, it is because he is ALSO a PYTHON developer…). While the barrier of entry is arguably permeable, developers idealy have a software engineering background (Before I get stones thrown at me…. I’ve seen great developers with accounting background!)

Software 2.0 is very different. Trying to bridge developers over this domain is hard. This is a new field. Typically dominated by data scientists. Statistics and weights are at the center of everything.

But software 3.0 is back at programming. I don’t want in any way undermine the knowledge and experience required to make applications here, but calling a LLM api and embbed in your application is rather trivial. Maybe calling a vector DB to perform a RAG scenario is also achievable. These are simple scenarios but nevertheless, scenarios that are often the current use cases we see. More advanced scenarios exist and I will try to give my view on how this can optimally work.

 

Software 1.0 - Classic development

Tech background, Ideally Software engineering

Sofware 2.0 - Neural Networks - LLMs

ML and Deep Learning, NLP - Data Scientists

Software 3.0 - GenAI Embbeded applications

Tech background, Ideally Software engineering WITH ADDITION of GenAI development techniques

 

Who gets to work on some GenAI use cases

 

Level of skills by use case

Simple CAP/RAP Application leveraging SAP GenAI tools

Advanced CAP/RAP Application leveraging SAP GenAI tools

Advanced CAP/RAP Application leveraging GenAI Tools AND/OR Other platforms (Hybrid model)

Application Logic (CAP/RAP)

SAP Developer

SAP Developer

SAP Developer

Modeling in SAP GenAI HUB - LLMs + Prompts etc

SAP Developer

SAP Developer

SAP Developer for basic external platform modelling, if has this knowledge (Langchain, for example)

and/or

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

Simple RAG Scenario in SAP BTP

SAP Developer

N/A

N/A

Advanced RAG Techniques

N/A

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

Simple Agentic systems

N/A

SAP Developer

SAP Developer for basic external platform modelling, if has this knowledge (Agentic frameworks in langchain for example)

and/or

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

Agent Skills/Tools

N/A

SAP Developer

SAP Developer for basic external platform modelling, if has this knowledge (Agentic frameworks in langchain for example)

and/or

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

Advanced Agentic systems and teams of agents

N/A

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

chain-of-thought, reasoning, Long/short-term memory

N/A

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

More Experienced Software 3.0 developer - GenAI Developer?

Let's get the conversation going. Post you comments here.

entry.title: How to import a BC app based on RAP/SMBC/TOBJ with abapGit
entry.content:
Introduction

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a business configuration maintenance object (SMBC), a transport object (TOBJ) is generated by default and used in the behavior implementation.

While the community version of abapGit supports export/import of TOBJ, this is not the case in the pre-installed cloud version of abapGit.

This blog explains what you can do as a workaround when cloning a Git repository with TOBJ to an ABAP platform system with preinstalled cloud version of abapGit.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Workaround

The only way to create a TOBJ in BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public is to generate an SMBC object using the ADT wizard.

  1. Create a dummy table that meets the prerequisites of the wizard.
  2. Run the wizard. In the "BC Administration" section of the configuration screen, change the name of the transport object to the name of the TOBJ of the Git repository.
  3. Finish the wizard
  4. Adjust the generated TOBJ to match the one from the Git repository.
  5. Assign the generated TOBJ to the correct package
  6. Delete all other generated repository objects including the dummy table

entry.title: How to Set up Custom Code Migration App on BTP using CIAS BTP Service
entry.content:
OVERVIEW

Automated setup and configuration in SAP BTP, ABAP environment and Cloud Integration Automation Service application subscription

Technically based on remote ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

Analysis of custom code for SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, ABAP Cloud,

 Simplified custom code remediation

Obsolete custom code removal based on usage data

Identification of custom code candidates for back-to-standard and redesign Evaluation of custom code for semi-automated adaptation (quick fixes)

Available in SAP BTP, ABAP environment

SAP BTP Cloud Integration Automation 

This service provides you a guided workflow to integrate SAP cloud solutions to on-premises and other SAP cloud solutions. The guided workflow contains instructions for manual and automated tasks to enable an easy and quick integration configuration setup and it multiple advantages as below:

  • It provides the User and task centric approach in integration configuration scenarios, using guided workflows.
  • Planning capabilities to plan an integration scenario
  • The guided workflow covers an integration setup for a specific scenario.
  • Instructions are provided in a task format and can be assigned to a dedicated role.
  • Instructions are provided based on your landscape and the chosen integration scenario.
  • Automated technical configuration steps on selected targets are available and enabled.
  • Monitoring capabilities for the integration scenario guided workflow.

Supported Scenario for the CIAS on CCM Application  :

 

 

System Preparation: Below step need to be executing as initial setup :

  1. Preparing the SAP Business Suite System:
  • Creating the SAP Note Analyzer Report
  • Downloading the Required SAP Notes
  • Implementing the Required SAP Notes
  • Activating the ADT Service Node
  • Creating a System User for the RFC Destination
  • Assigning Permissions to the System User
  1. Preparing the Subaccount for On-Premise Connectivity:
  • Creating a New Subaccount in SAP BTP
  • Providing the Details of the Subaccount
  • Increasing the Quota for the Subaccount
  • Creating the Destination Service Instance
  • Assigning Permissions to the Connection User
  • Connecting the Cloud Connector to the Subaccount
  • Configuring Access Control (RFC) with Load Balancing
  • Configuring Access Control (RFC) without Load Balancing
  • Creating the RFC Destination with Load Balancing
  • Creating the RFC Destination without Load Balancing
  1. Preparing the ABAP Environment
  • Subscribing to the Web Access for ABAP
  • Setting up the ABAP Environment
  • Providing the Details of the ABAP Environment
  • Creating a Business User for Custom Code Migration
  • Enabling the Custom Code Migration Application
  • Integrating the Destination Service Instance
  • Maintaining the CCM Communication Arrangement
  1. Testing the Custom Code Migration
  • Running the Custom Code Migration (ABAP Environment)
  • Running the Custom Code Migration (SAP S/4HANA)

Adding new system for Custom Code Assessment:

Step 1: Add the RFC Protocol for the backend system in Cloud Connector with sap gateway  port as below and import the list of scenarios as required:

Step2 : Create the RFC Destination in the BTP Subaccount

Step 3: We need to define the Select the SAP Business Suit System which is optional and Select the BTP Application ID in the Subscription

 Steps 4: Once we generate the Workflow for Configuring the System Component which includes :

  • Access Option: Can be Via (SAP Logon, Host, Message Server and Router String)
  • Define your Message Server , Host Name
  • External SAP System Name
  • Client number, SID, Instance Number, Logon Group and the installation number .
  • Provide the Cloud connector URL and BTP Subaccount URL

Step 5 : Add the Roles and assign to the users :

 

Step 6 : Define the scope scenario for SAP S/4HANA Migration with Without load balancing

Steps Perform the Smoke Test Run in Web On ABAP Environment :

  • Create SAP S/4HANA Project in CCM Application:
  •  
  • ￼

     

  • Run the Analysis for the system to be analyzed:
  •  

  •  

 

 

  • Now we can check the number the Analyzes findings and validate:

 

  •  

    Conclusion: 
  • Custom Code Migration on BTP saves costs for an extra system and provides permanent access to the new features. Collected usage data can be considered for identification and removal of unused code. Advanced analysis capabilities comprise various dashboards and graphics. Top custom code TCO drivers can be detected as candidates for redesign instead of adaptation

Cloud Integration Automation Service | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Free SAP BTP - Free ABAP System instance
entry.content:
This article will show how you can create a Free ABAP environment.

After all, run the SFlight loading ABAP program to load data into the Hana database tables.

To accomplish all these and you can follow without fear, it will have a lot of print screens, so, long article.

At your Global account level, select the Boosters section.

In the filter, search for ABAP:

Calm down!! 🧘,  I know you want to click on "Prepare an Account for ABAP Development (Free-tier)" but, there is a requirement for SAP BTP ABAP Environment,  "Landscape Portal". 

We need to create it first, selecting the "Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment (Free-Tier)".

We will hit 2 or 3 walls, sending us back a few steps, but I will help with screens shots! 

Click on the tile, it will open a section showing the description, components, resources, bla bla bla... Press the start

 

In the coming screen, the booster will check settings a permission, it should show up like below:

If it is not like mine (all green and done), follow the instructions on how to fix the authorizations and entitlements and come back here.

Clicking NEXT should show a screen like this one. 

NOTE: If you want to create it manually without using the Booster, notice this trick.... the landscape portal not shows up in the list of entitlements!! in order to show up in the list, you need to edit the entitlements and add it.

After the deployment finishes, now it is time to create ABAP instance. Go to Global account and select boosters, then filter by ABAP. Click on tile "Prepare an Account for ABAP Development (Free-Tier)"

After you read all info (Overview, Components,Additional), click on start. If there are some Authorization or Entitlements missing, fix them first. ( the entitlement error can be fixed adding it like shown up in the manual creation of Landscape portal)

In the next screen, we already created the Subaccount, so, don't need to create new one.

select proper Subaccount and space. Next screen is auto explanatory.

Choose the ABAP system id and review your choices.

The creation will take a few hours, it's normal / common. Stay calm 🧘 and after it finishes, you will receive an email like this one.

Congratulations!! 🎊 Now you have your personal ABAP Cloud Environment (the best of it, it's FREEEEEEEEE)

entry.title: RAP: Augmentation
entry.content:
Augmentation:

  • used to enhance the modify request, before it reaches transactional buffer.
  • augmentation is available for projection bdefs only.
  • the augment methods are called before the create, update method.

Note:

  • it is not possible to set a field in augment which was already set for the original instance. For example, values set in the original request cannot be changed.  Only fields which are unset in the original request can be added.
  • it is not possible to set fields in augment that are defined as read only .

The following operations can be augmented:

  • create
  • update
  • create by association

ABAP EML - MODIFY AUGMENTING ENTITY:

In the augmentation method, it's not recommended to use read, modify eml, as the data has not been moved into the transactional buffer yet. so we need to use a special variant of modify entity, entities, which is only possible in the context of projections bo.

  • for the eml statements, no response parameters can be specified, i.e., the additions failed, mapped, or reported cannot be used.
  • an abap eml modify statement using the addition augmenting must not overwrite fields of the original request. if done, a dump will be thrown.

Flow of augmentation method in interactive phase:

Difference between augmentation and determination:

Both Augmentation and determination are similar concept, as both are used to modify the request based on some standard trigger condition. But here are some differences.

Steps to develop precheck:

 1. Add precheck in standard operation in bdef projection.

2. Implement the logic for precheck in abap behavior pool.

Use case:

Requirement: If the user doesn’t provides value for field “name”, Then it should be assigned with an default value “test”, in modify request.

 

To achieve this, we have added a augmentation in the update operation. Before executing the update method, the entry will beassigned with default value in augmentation method. In the Augmentation method, If the name is empty, we fill default value name using modify augmentation eml.

Code for augmentation in abap behavior pool:

 

Entities importing parameter:
